comment: DESI DR2 (2025, arXiv:2503.14738) CPL w0-wa best fits for DESI BAO + CMB + one supernova compilation at a time. The three SN compilations (Pantheon+, Union3, DESY5) OVERLAP heavily (shared low-z SNe) and are ALTERNATIVE datasets, not independent -- they must NOT be stacked/multiplied. rho is the published-typical (w0,wa) CPL correlation.

tfpt_prediction.note: cosmological constant: w=-1 exactly (Lambda/H0 engine, S_dS rho_Lambda = 32 pi^4)

overlap_note: Pantheon+, Union3 and DES-SN5YR share substantial low-z SNe; their w0-wa constraints are correlated, so combining them as if independent (e.g. sqrt(sum of sigma^2)) manufactures a spurious >5 sigma. Headline = strongest single overlap-aware combination, not a product.

status_timeline.entry: baseline: DESI DR2 (arXiv:2503.14738) frozen combinations above; strongest overlap-aware exclusion of w=-1 is DESI+CMB+DES-SN5YR at 4.2 sigma published (~4.4 sigma reproduced).
2026 direction, recorded BEFORE DESI DR3: the evolving-DE preference is DISSOLVING, exactly as TFPT's w=-1 fixed point predicts.

des_dovekie_recalibration.reference: DES collab., arXiv:2511.07517, MNRAS (2026) -- DES-Dovekie recalibration of DES-SN5YR
des_dovekie_recalibration.note: found an input-file error in DES-SN5YR: the 9 SALT3 calibration-variant weights were rounded to 0.3 instead of 1/sqrt(9)=0.33, so the calibration-systematic weights summed to 0.81 instead of 1, underestimating the total photometric uncertainty by ~20%. Full recalibration (new cross-calibration incl. HST white dwarfs + Gaia, retrained SALT3).
des_dovekie_recalibration.significance_note: 3.2 sigma, reduced from 4.2 sigma for DES-SN5YR; equivalent to Bayesian odds ~5:1 -- 'only a weak preference for evolving dark energy' (paper's own wording)

bayesian_reanalysis.reference: Ong, Yallup & Handley, arXiv:2603.05472 (2026) -- nested-sampling evidences (PolyChord), Ockham/Jeffreys-Lindley penalty
bayesian_reanalysis.desi_dr2_cmb_only_note: the DESI collaboration's 3.1 sigma frequentist preference for w0waCDM from DESI DR2 + CMB alone is ELIMINATED: ln B = -0.57 +- 0.26, modestly favouring LCDM
bayesian_reanalysis.desy5_note: with the ORIGINAL DES-SN5YR calibration the preference survives (ln B = +3.32 +- 0.27, 3.07 +- 0.10 sigma) -- traced to the calibration error, which put DES-SN5YR in 2.95 sigma conflict with DESI DR2 within LCDM (1.96 sigma after correction). 'With the calibration corrected, the Bayesian evidence for dynamical dark energy vanishes.'
bayesian_reanalysis.union3_note: Union3-driven pulls REMAIN: published frequentist 3.8 sigma (DESI DR2 + CMB + Union3, not recalibrated); Bayesian ln B = +1.37 +- 0.27 (2.23 +- 0.15 sigma) for the same triplet

status_timeline.surviving_pulls: strongest surviving frequentist exclusions of w=-1: corrected DES-Dovekie combination 3.2 sigma (published); Union3 combination 3.8 sigma frequentist but only 2.23 sigma Bayesian. The 2025 4.2-4.4 sigma headline is now known to be calibration-inflated.
